page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is bundled with 1843 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate solid mid-range performance across most workloads. The server achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48717.7 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15064.0. It demonstrates strong capabilities in specific areas, such as image processing (14001.0 on Geekbench) and encryption (43321.1 on Passmark), both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Memory performance is also notable, with a Passmark memory score of 2757.12 and cached read bandwidth reaching 2502.0 GB/sec.

While the instance handles small-scale LLM inference efficiently, achieving 275.95 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model, it exhibits a clear performance tradeoff on larger models, dropping to a weak 2.74 tokens/sec for 70B model text generation. Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio, dedicated CPU allocation, and substantial local storage, this server is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, database operations, software compilation, and moderate web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It offers 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores), 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it delivers balanced performance with a Passmark CPU score of 48717.7 and a strong Passmark memory score of 2757.12. Benchmark data highlights strong capabilities in image processing and encryption, alongside efficient small-model LLM inference. However, a notable performance tradeoff occurs with large 70B LLM text generation, which is weak at 2.74 tokens/sec. This instance is highly suitable for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, delivering balanced compute and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, optimized for databases and enterprise apps.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and substantial local storage for enterprise applications.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB RAM provide a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Includes 1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong performance in encryption, image processing, and memory operations
Tradeoff in large-scale LLM inference with weak 70B model text generation
Suitable for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
